536	def _plan_expert_facet_queries(
537	    question: str,
538	    llm: LlmFn,
539	    per_query_retrieve: PerQueryRetrieveFn,
540	    *,
541	    max_queries: int | None = None,
542	    retrieve_kwargs: dict | None = None,
543	    retrieval_deadline_monotonic: "float | None" = None,
544	    retrieval_plan: Any = None,
545	    retrieval_policy: Any = None,
546	) -> tuple[list[str], list[Any]]:
547	    """R1+R2 (I-deepfix-001, #1344) facet-driven frontier: seed queries from the expert-facet tree,
548	    issue them directly, then (when R2 is enabled) run the facet-completeness expansion loop over the
549	    UNCOVERED facets until the source yield saturates. Returns (queries_issued, per_query_results),
550	    the SAME contract as ``plan_fs_researcher_queries``.
551	
552	    R1 widens the frontier (facet x angle, scope-anchored) — the largest single DRB-II recall lever.
553	    R2 keys the completeness/expansion loop to the TASK's own facets so a general (non-clinical) task
554	    no longer reads a vacuous "0 of 0" and fires real gap-closing retrieval. Both ADD on-topic queries
555	    only and DROP ZERO sources; the FS-Researcher ``max_queries`` cap (compute-safety) still bounds
556	    cost; the R2 saturation stop is yield-keyed, never a breadth count (§-1.3).
557	    """
558	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import expert_facet_planner as _efp
559	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import facet_completeness as _fc
560	
561	    max_queries = max_queries or _max_queries()
562	    retrieve_kwargs = dict(retrieve_kwargs or {})
563	
564	    queries: list[str] = []
565	    results: list[Any] = []
566	    seen_q: set[str] = set()
567	    corpus_rows: list[dict] = []
568	
569	    _deadline = retrieval_deadline_monotonic
570	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344): seconds of wall-time consumed by the ADDITIVE landmark-study
571	    # planning lane, credited BACK to the wall so that lane rides OUTSIDE the baseline retrieval budget
572	    # and never displaces a baseline query (Codex/Fable P1 "or add budget"). A mutable box so the nested
573	    # ``_wall_passed`` reads the running total; it stays 0.0 (byte-identical) unless the landmark lane fires.
574	    _extra_lane_credit = [0.0]
575	
576	    def _wall_passed() -> bool:
577	        _d = _deadline
578	        if _d is not None and _extra_lane_credit[0]:
579	            _d = _d + _extra_lane_credit[0]
580	        return _retrieval_deadline_passed(_d)
581	
582	    if _wall_passed():
583	        return queries, results
584	
585	    # R1: build the facet tree (one bounded LLM call) and its angle queries. The
586	    # contract's SCOPE is carried into the frontier by the projection's amplified
587	    # queries (`_projection_amplified_queries`, prepended below) — NOT by rewriting
588	    # facet query text — so the champion facet frontier stays a byte-identical
589	    # subset and the superset invariant holds. `retrieval_policy` therefore drives
590	    # the NEGATIVE-predicate / backend-filter view (exclusions, date interval,
591	    # quality), never the positive facet query text.
592	    facets = _efp.plan_expert_facets(question, llm)
593	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3a (#1344): expert-facet-planner ACTIVATION fire marker. Emitted ONLY when
594	    # PG_EXPERT_FACET_PLANNER is ON (this whole facet path is reached only under the flag; the guard keeps
595	    # the marker OFF byte-identical even if a test drives this helper directly). facets=0 with the flag ON
596	    # is the eligible-yet-zero (degenerate-LLM-reply) signal. Structural presence + count (§-1.3).
597	    if _efp.expert_facet_enabled():
598	        logger.info("[activation] expert_facet_planner: facets=%d", len(facets))
599	
600	    # R1+R2 seed/reserve split (I-deepfix-001, #1344). Seed BREADTH-FIRST (every facet's
601	    # primary angle before any facet's deeper angle) so the query budget spreads across ALL
602	    # facets — fixing the measured "only 7 of 35 facets seeded" starvation. When the R2
603	    # completeness loop is ON, seed only the first `_seed_angles_per_facet()` angle(s) per
604	    # facet and RESERVE each facet's remaining angles for the R2 expansion loop to fire on
605	    # the facets the seed corpus leaves UNCOVERED. The prior code registered EVERY angle as a
606	    # seed, so `run_facet_expansion` (drawing from the SAME `facet.queries`) always read
607	    # "frontier exhausted" and issued ZERO expansion queries — the Codex/Fable P1. When R2 is
608	    # OFF the full angle frontier is seeded (the R1-only path is unchanged in count).
609	    _r2_on = _fc.facet_completeness_enabled()
610	    _seed_angle_limit = _seed_angles_per_facet() if _r2_on else None
611	    seed_queries = _breadth_first_seeds(facets, _seed_angle_limit)
612	
613	    # S2 (planning gate): PREPEND the gate projection's scope-anchored amplified
614	    # queries to the facet frontier and RAISE the budget by the net-new gate slice
615	    # — exactly like the sub_entity / landmark widen lanes — so a gate lane
616	    # (journal-only / English / a mandatory topic) is issued from the FIRST query
617	    # and NEVER swaps out a baseline facet query. This is the no-starvation core:
618	    # the gate's scope reaches the seed frontier BEFORE any fetch, additively. []
619	    # (default / OFF / no projection) => byte-identical facet frontier.
620	    _gate_qs = _projection_amplified_queries(retrieval_plan, question)
621	    if _gate_qs:
622	        _seed_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in seed_queries}
623	        _new_gate = [q for q in _gate_qs if (q or "").strip().lower() not in _seed_keys]
624	        if _new_gate:
625	            # gate lanes lead; budget rises by the net-new slice (never displaces).
626	            seed_queries = list(_new_gate) + list(seed_queries)
627	            max_queries = max_queries + len(_new_gate)
628	        logger.info(
629	            "[activation] planning_gate_projection: amplified_queries=%d", len(_new_gate)
630	        )
631	
632	    # R5 (I-deepfix-001, #1344): multilingual / cross-lingual frontier. On a
633	    # non-English (e.g. zh) DRB-II task the English facet queries never reach the
634	    # native-language primaries; detect the task's language profile and ADD
635	    # on-language queries (English stays first + unchanged) so a native-language
636	    # source and its English paraphrase land in the SAME multi-backend retrieval,
637	    # cross-lingually reranked into one consolidation basket. English-only tasks
638	    # are byte-identical (the expansion returns the seeds unchanged); default-ON,
639	    # OFF via PG_MULTILINGUAL_RETRIEVAL. §-1.3: adds on-language queries only,
640	    # drops ZERO sources, touches no faithfulness gate.
641	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import language_profile as _lp
642	    if _lp.multilingual_enabled():
643	        _profile = _lp.detect_language_profile(question)
644	        if _profile.is_multilingual:
645	            # Inject a production translator wrapping the GLM policy so an EXPLICIT
646	            # -language task (all-ASCII English body + "Answer in Chinese") — which has
647	            # no native script to carry — still emits real native-language queries.
648	            _translate_fn = _make_multilingual_translate_fn(
649	                llm, list(seed_queries), _profile.non_english
650	            )
651	            _expanded = _lp.expand_queries_for_profile(
652	                seed_queries, _profile, question, translate_fn=_translate_fn
653	            )
654	            # Separate the native additions from the English seeds, then RESERVE a
655	            # bounded slice of native queries inside the query budget so a wide English
656	            # R1 frontier cannot starve the task's own language out of the issued set.
657	            _eng_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in seed_queries}
658	            _native_adds = [
659	                q for q in _expanded if (q or "").strip().lower() not in _eng_keys
660	            ]
661	            seed_queries = _reserve_native_within_budget(
662	                list(seed_queries), _native_adds, max_queries
663	            )
664	
665	    # R2 entity-qgen (I-deepfix-001, #1344): STORM-style sub-entity + perspective query expansion.
666	    # The abstract facet frontier names sub-TOPICS x analytical angles but never the concrete NICHE
667	    # sub-entities a DRB-II rubric names (specific occupations / sectors / demographic groups), so
668	    # their profession-specific primaries are never fetched and the corpus stays canonical-only. This
669	    # AUGMENTS (does not replace) the frontier: ONE bounded LLM call enumerates the named sub-entities
670	    # + deterministic STORM-style disciplinary perspective lenses, each scope-anchored to the question
671	    # so it cannot drift off-subject, and the bounded sub-entity slice is ADDED ON TOP of the FULL
672	    # baseline frontier — the effective query budget is RAISED by the added slice so the sub-entity
673	    # queries never SWAP OUT a baseline query (the Codex/Fable iter-1 REVISE). Every discovered source
674	    # flows through the UNCHANGED per_query_retrieve + frozen faithfulness engine — NEVER auto-trusted.
675	    # Default OFF (PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_EXPANSION) => byte-identical. §-1.3: adds on-topic queries only,
676	    # drops ZERO sources, touches no faithfulness gate.
677	    #
678	    # Placed AFTER the R5 block (Codex/Fable iter-2 P1 fix). When R2 ran BEFORE R5 it lengthened
679	    # `seed_queries` first, and `language_profile.expand_queries_for_profile` caps its native-language
680	    # additions at max(PG_MULTILINGUAL_MAX_QUERIES, len(base)); once the R2 slice pushed `base` over
681	    # that cap the native-language queries a flag-OFF run WOULD issue were DROPPED — breaking the §-1.3
682	    # strict-superset on non-English (e.g. zh) tasks. Running R5 first means R5 expands the PRE-R2
683	    # baseline — the IDENTICAL input the flag-OFF path expands — into `seed_queries` (English + reserved
684	    # native). `widen_with_sub_entities` then keeps that whole R5-expanded window at the front and only
685	    # RAISES the budget by the sub-entity slice, so the flag-ON issued set is a strict SUPERSET of the
686	    # flag-OFF set: every native-language query is STILL issued (zero dropped) AND the sub-entity queries
687	    # are added. The sub-entity queries are English scope-anchored and route through the unchanged fetch
688	    # un-translated — option-(b): R5 native widening + R2 sub-entity widening both land on the budget;
689	    # translating the sub-entity slice is deliberately NOT done (it would complicate the superset for no
690	    # required gain). Dedup is against the CURRENT (R5-expanded) frontier so no query is issued twice.
691	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import sub_entity_query_expander as _sqe
692	    if _sqe.sub_entity_expansion_enabled() and not _wall_passed():
693	        _sub_qs = _sqe.plan_sub_entity_queries(question, llm)
694	        _new_sub_count = 0
695	        if _sub_qs:
696	            _seed_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in seed_queries}
697	            _new_sub = [q for q in _sub_qs if (q or "").strip().lower() not in _seed_keys]
698	            _new_sub_count = len(_new_sub)
699	            if _new_sub:
700	                seed_queries, max_queries = _sqe.widen_with_sub_entities(
701	                    list(seed_queries), _new_sub, max_queries
702	                )
703	        # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3a (#1344): sub-entity-expansion ACTIVATION fire marker. Emitted ONLY inside
704	        # this PG_SUBENTITY_QUERY_EXPANSION-gated block => OFF byte-identical. expanded_queries = the NET
705	        # NEW sub-entity queries added on top of the current frontier (0 = LLM named none / all duplicates,
706	        # the eligible-yet-zero signal). Structural presence + count (§-1.3).
707	        logger.info("[activation] subentity_query_expansion: expanded_queries=%d", _new_sub_count)
708	
709	    # COV-C landmark-study expander (I-deepfix-001 Wave-3, #1344): the abstract facet + sub-entity
710	    # frontier never names the LANDMARK empirical studies / RCTs / seminal datasets central to the
711	    # question (the question carries no author names), so the empirical CORE stays absent. ONE bounded
712	    # LLM call enumerates the IN-WINDOW landmark studies (constrained to the question's stated
713	    # publication ceiling `date_end_iso` so a "before June 2023" question seeds the pre-print /
714	    # working-paper version, NEVER a later re-publication) and each scope-anchored query is ADDED ON
715	    # TOP of the FULL baseline frontier — the budget is RAISED by the added slice so a landmark query
716	    # never SWAPS OUT a baseline query (mirrors sub_entity). Every discovered source flows through the
717	    # UNCHANGED per_query_retrieve + frozen faithfulness engine — NEVER auto-trusted. Default OFF
718	    # (PG_LANDMARK_EXPANDER) => byte-identical. §-1.3: adds on-topic in-window queries only, drops ZERO
719	    # sources, touches no faithfulness gate.
720	    # The env flag is read via ``_landmark_expander_enabled()`` (reads PG_LANDMARK_EXPANDER directly,
721	    # NO import) so a flag-OFF run never imports ``landmark_study_expander`` — the OFF path is
722	    # byte-identical even if that module is absent/broken (Codex/Fable Wave-3 P0 defense-in-depth). The
723	    # module import is DEFERRED inside the guard for the same reason.
724	    if _landmark_expander_enabled() and not _wall_passed():
725	        _lm_t0 = time.monotonic()
726	        _new_lm_count = 0
727	        _lm_failed_open = False  # Wave-3b (#1344, Codex P1.1): distinguishes RAN-ok from FAIL-OPEN for the marker
728	        # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344) — Codex/Fable P1 (additive-on-failure): the ENTIRE landmark lane
729	        # (deferred module import + window extraction + plan + widen) is FAIL-OPEN. If
730	        # ``landmark_study_expander`` is absent or broken, or its bounded LLM planning call raises, the
731	        # lane adds ZERO queries and qgen proceeds on the unchanged baseline frontier — it must NEVER
732	        # abort the whole query generation. This IS the additive / faithfulness-neutral contract: an
733	        # additive coverage lane can only ADD sources, so ANY failure degrades to the flag-OFF
734	        # (zero-added) behaviour, never a hard stop (§-1.3). Also directly covers the Wave-3 P0 concern
735	        # that the FORCE-ON path could ModuleNotFoundError at run time: a missing module now no-ops.
736	        try:
737	            from src.polaris_graph.retrieval import landmark_study_expander as _lse
738	            try:
739	                from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.intake_constraint_extractor import (
740	                    extract_constraints_regex as _extract_constraints_regex,
741	                )
742	                _window_end = _extract_constraints_regex(question).date_end_iso()
743	            except Exception:
744	                _window_end = None
745	            _lm_qs = _lse.plan_landmark_study_queries(question, llm, _window_end)
746	            if _lm_qs:
747	                _seed_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in seed_queries}
748	                _new_lm = [q for q in _lm_qs if (q or "").strip().lower() not in _seed_keys]
749	                _new_lm_count = len(_new_lm)
750	                if _new_lm:
751	                    seed_queries, max_queries = _lse.widen_with_landmark_studies(
752	                        list(seed_queries), _new_lm, max_queries
753	                    )
754	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive lane: any failure adds ZERO, never aborts qgen
755	            # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3b (#1344, Codex P1.1): a FAIL-OPEN landmark lane must NOT read as a
756	            # healthy fire. Set the degrade flag, emit a DISTINCT ``unavailable_failopen`` marker, and
757	            # SUPPRESS the positive ``expanded_queries=N`` marker on this path (below). The run_gate_b
758	            # landmark canary registers this degrade literal as an absent_marker, so a crashed/missing/
759	            # not-recovered expander is REJECTED as dark instead of passing as a healthy added-zero fire.
760	            # The lane STILL fails open (adds zero, never aborts qgen); only the LIVENESS LOG is made
761	            # honest — a lane that FAILED to run is no longer indistinguishable from one that RAN and
762	            # legitimately added zero (§-1.3 anti-dark, faithfulness-neutral).
763	            _lm_failed_open = True
764	            logger.warning(
765	                "[activation] landmark_study_expansion: unavailable_failopen (added 0)", exc_info=True
766	            )
767	            _new_lm_count = 0
768	        # Credit the landmark planning time BACK to the wall so this additive lane rides OUTSIDE the
769	        # baseline budget — the baseline seed frontier issued below (and the R2 completeness loop) get
770	        # the same wall time they would with the lane OFF, so no baseline query is displaced by the
771	        # landmark enumeration's inline LLM call (Codex/Fable P1 "or add budget").
772	        _extra_lane_credit[0] += max(0.0, time.monotonic() - _lm_t0)
773	        # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344) landmark-expansion ACTIVATION fire marker, Wave-3b (Codex P1.1)
774	        # honesty split. Emitted ONLY inside this PG_LANDMARK_EXPANDER-gated block => OFF byte-identical,
775	        # and ONLY when the lane RAN successfully (``not _lm_failed_open``). expanded_queries = the NET
776	        # NEW in-window landmark queries added on top of the current frontier; 0 here means the expander
777	        # RAN and legitimately added zero (LLM named none / all duplicates / no in-window study) — the
778	        # ACCEPTED eligible-yet-zero signal (§-1.3). On the FAIL-OPEN path this positive marker is
779	        # SUPPRESSED and the ``unavailable_failopen`` degrade marker (above) fires instead, so the canary
780	        # can tell a lane that RAN-and-added-zero (accept) from one that FAILED-and-added-zero (reject as
781	        # dark). Structural presence + count.
782	        if not _lm_failed_open:
783	            logger.info("[activation] landmark_study_expansion: expanded_queries=%d", _new_lm_count)
784	
785	    # GENERAL stance / view-diversification seed lane (default OFF, PG_STANCE_DIVERSIFY_SEEDS): a GENERAL
786	    # deep-research technique — a good report on ANY contested topic must cover multiple viewpoints. For
787	    # each planned facet ALSO issue a small set of queries framing the facet from distinct STANCES
788	    # (supporting / opposing / challenges / opportunities), each scope-anchored to the question. Generic
789	    # topic-agnostic templates only — NO study title / country / benchmark topic is baked in. UNLIKE the
790	    # sub-entity / landmark widen lanes this lane does NOT raise the budget: the net-new stance queries are
791	    # simply APPENDED to the seed frontier and compete for the SAME `max_queries` budget in the seed-issue
792	    # below — if the budget is already full they don't all fit (honest, never a raised cap to fit them).
793	    # De-duplicated against the CURRENT frontier here + against `seen_q` inside the seed-issue loop. §-1.3:
794	    # ADDS on-topic queries only, DROPS ZERO sources, CAPS/THINS/TARGETS NOTHING; every stance query routes
795	    # through the UNCHANGED per_query_retrieve + the FROZEN faithfulness engine — a stance query that finds
796	    # nothing verifiable simply contributes nothing (honest). FAIL-OPEN (Codex/Fable additive-on-failure):
797	    # any import/logic error adds ZERO queries and emits the DISTINCT `unavailable_failopen` degrade marker
798	    # — it NEVER aborts the host query-gen path. The healthy `issued=N` marker reports the REALIZED count of
799	    # net-new stance queries added to the frontier (0 = a legitimate ran-ok-zero — no facet / all duplicates
800	    # — and is ACCEPTED; NEVER gated on a count > 0, §-1.3). Default OFF => this block is skipped entirely =>
801	    # `seed_queries` unchanged => byte-identical.
802	    # Wave-6c (Codex P1): the healthy liveness marker is emitted AFTER _issue_seed_frontier below, keyed on the
803	    # REALIZED count of stance queries that ACTUALLY made it through the (deliberately-unraised) budget + dedup +
804	    # wall — NOT the pre-truncation appended count. Here we only APPEND the net-new stance queries + record their
805	    # keys so the realized count can be measured against the issued set.
806	    _stance_lane_active = False
807	    _stance_keys: set[str] = set()
808	    if _stance_diversify_enabled() and not _wall_passed():
809	        _stance_lane_active = True
810	        try:
811	            _stance_qs = _stance_diversify_seeds(facets, question)
812	            if _stance_qs:
813	                _seed_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in seed_queries}
814	                _new_stance = [q for q in _stance_qs if (q or "").strip().lower() not in _seed_keys]
815	                if _new_stance:
816	                    _stance_keys = {(q or "").strip().lower() for q in _new_stance}
817	                    # ADD ONLY — append to the frontier; `max_queries` is UNCHANGED (never raised to fit
818	                    # them), so the stance queries flow through the SAME budget-bounded seed-issue below
819	                    # (§-1.3: additive-only, no cap/target — if the budget is full they don't all fit).
820	                    seed_queries = list(seed_queries) + _new_stance
821	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive lane: any failure adds ZERO, never aborts qgen
822	            # A FAIL-OPEN stance lane must NOT read as a healthy fire: clear the active flag (SUPPRESSES the
823	            # positive issued= marker below) and emit the DISTINCT `unavailable_failopen` marker so the
824	            # run_gate_b activation canary (which registers the degrade literal as an absent_marker) REJECTS
825	            # the dark lane instead of passing it as a healthy added-zero. The lane STILL fails open (adds
826	            # zero, never aborts qgen); only the liveness LOG is made honest (§-1.3 anti-dark).
827	            _stance_lane_active = False
828	            _stance_keys = set()
829	            logger.warning(
830	                "[activation] stance_diversify_seeds: unavailable_failopen (added 0)", exc_info=True
831	            )
832	
833	    # Issue the seed frontier (facet-angle queries are already full queries — no per-todo llm()
834	    # derivation needed). Record ONLY the queries ACTUALLY issued in the shared seen-set: a
835	    # budget-truncated seed stays eligible for the R2 loop, and the RESERVE angles (deliberately never
836	    # placed in `seed_queries`) stay OUT of `seen_q` so the expansion loop below can fire them for
837	    # still-uncovered facets. Bounded by the compute-safety query budget + the retrieval wall.
838	    # I-deepfix-001 Wave-3 (#1344): the fan-out is bounded-parallel when PG_QGEN_PARALLEL_QUERIES>1
839	    # (order-stable merge, SAME query set); serial + byte-identical at the default 1.
840	    _issued, _issued_results, _issued_rows = _issue_seed_frontier(
841	        seed_queries, seen_q, max_queries - len(queries),
842	        per_query_retrieve, retrieve_kwargs, _wall_passed,
843	    )
844	    queries.extend(_issued)
845	    results.extend(_issued_results)
846	    corpus_rows.extend(_issued_rows)
847	
848	    # Wave-6c (Codex P1): emit the stance-lane liveness marker with the REALIZED issued count — how many
849	    # net-new stance queries ACTUALLY passed the (deliberately-unraised) budget + dedup + wall in
850	    # _issue_seed_frontier, counted from `_issued` (the verbatim issued queries), NOT the pre-truncation
851	    # appended count. Mirrors the qgen_parallel_fanout issued= contract. Suppressed on the fail-open path
852	    # (which logs unavailable_failopen instead). issued=0 is an honest ran-ok-zero — NEVER gated on >0 (§-1.3).
853	    if _stance_lane_active:
854	        _realized_stance = sum(1 for _q in _issued if (_q or "").strip().lower() in _stance_keys)
855	        logger.info("[activation] stance_diversify_seeds: issued=%d", _realized_stance)
856	
857	    # R2: the facet-completeness expansion loop closes gaps on UNCOVERED facets until yield saturates.
858	    if _fc.facet_completeness_enabled() and facets and len(queries) < max_queries and not _wall_passed():
859	        expansion = _fc.run_facet_expansion(
860	            facets,
861	            corpus_rows,
862	            per_query_retrieve,
863	            retrieve_kwargs=retrieve_kwargs,
864	            max_queries=max_queries - len(queries),
865	            already_issued=seen_q,
866	            retrieval_deadline_passed=_wall_passed,
867	        )
868	        queries.extend(expansion.expansion_queries)
869	        results.extend(expansion.results)
870	
871	    return queries, results
872	
873	
874	def _projection_amplified_queries(retrieval_plan: Any, question: str) -> list[str]:
875	    """The gate projection's scope-anchored amplified queries (S2), or [] when
876	    there is no projection. Fail-open: any error yields [] so the FS frontier
877	    is exactly the champion frontier (the OFF path is byte-identical even if the
878	    projection is malformed). This is ADDITIVE — it only ever ADDS queries."""
879	    if retrieval_plan is None:
880	        return []
881	    try:
882	        return list(retrieval_plan.to_amplified_queries(base_question=question))
883	    except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — additive lane: any failure adds ZERO queries
884	        logger.warning(
885	            "[activation] planning_gate_projection: unavailable_failopen (added 0)",
886	            exc_info=True,
887	        )
888	        return []
889	
890	
891	def plan_fs_researcher_queries(
892	    question: str,
893	    llm: LlmFn,
894	    per_query_retrieve: PerQueryRetrieveFn,
895	    *,
896	    max_queries: int | None = None,
897	    max_rounds: int | None = None,
898	    retrieve_kwargs: dict | None = None,
899	    retrieval_deadline_monotonic: "float | None" = None,
900	    retrieval_plan: Any = None,
901	    retrieval_policy: Any = None,
902	) -> tuple[list[str], list[Any]]:
903	    """Run the FS-Researcher TOC/todo-queue + 6-item-checklist loop; return
904	    (queries_issued, per_query_results).
905	
906	    ``retrieval_policy`` (spec item 3): the typed, hash-stamped
907	    :class:`~planning.retrieval_projection.RetrievalPolicy` compiled from the
908	    pinned contract (allowed/excluded source kinds, date interval, languages,
909	    named in/exclusions, quality profile ref, per-predicate force). ``None``
910	    (default / PG_GATE OFF) => byte-identical champion behavior. When present it
911	    is the DECLARATIVE predicate set the backend-native filter + post-fetch
912	    citable-eligibility verdict read — NEVER folded into positive query text
913	    (exclusions stay negative). Derived from ``retrieval_plan`` when the caller
914	    passes only the projection.
915	
916	    index.md TOC: deconstruct the question into sub-topics (a todo queue). Each round: for every
917	    todo, derive ONE query and retrieve via the production `per_query_retrieve`; then a fixed 6-item
918	    self-review checklist (exhaustive coverage + information density) yields the deficient sub-topics
919	    that become the next round's todos. Stops on NONE or the query budget. Pure control flow over the
920	    injected llm/retrieve — no network or live_retriever import here.
921	
922	    I-deepfix-001 WALL-03 (#1344): ``retrieval_deadline_monotonic`` is the SHARED per-question
923	    retrieval wall (anchored ONCE by the spine, ``None`` when the wall knob is unset). FIX-2 already
924	    short-circuits the per-query ``per_query_retrieve`` once the wall passes; but the adaptive GLM
